evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent: setWebhook with secret_token, X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header verification, sendMessage/editMessageText/answerCallbackQuery, inline_keyboard nested arrays all match standard Bot API. No version date stated but API is stable. Long-tail handling: consistent throughout.
evidence.executability: Code split across multiple files (config.py, models.py, telegram_client.py, keyboards.py, main.py, tests/test_webhook.py) which extractor may concatenate. Pydantic Config uses allow_population_by_field_name (v1 syntax) — may break under Pydantic v2. main.py 'continued' blocks rely on imports/globals from prior fence (datetime imported inside continued block, Dict imported only in earlier client). Tests reference TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET which could be None. No sandbox-infeasibility patterns matched (mentions ngrok/Pinggy descriptively but not as required setup).
evidence.completeness: (a) BotFather acquisition described; (b) pip install bash block present; (c) .env template with exact var names; (d) backend code in fenced blocks; (e) no frontend (N/A for bot); (f) test command implicit (pytest) but no explicit '## Test' bash block with command. Multiple file paths in code comments may confuse extractor; env vars consistent.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Citations are bare numeric refs [20][2][9] with no URLs. No canonical core.telegram.org/bots/api URL anchored anywhere. No fabricated URLs but no official URLs either.

rubric_notes: Multi-file structure with file path comments is a common extractor hazard; deterministic script may write all blocks to single file. Pydantic v1 Config syntax (allow_population_by_field_name, extra='ignore' as class attrs) is a real freshness concern under Pydantic v2 but not in the deterministic trap list.
